body:
Full CSCV shipped: SLS(83) -> SLS(84)/9.98 (WARRANT#79).

This is the real Bailey & Lopez de Prado (2014) algorithm now, not the simplified proxy from before. Built as a COMPLETELY SEPARATE, independent search (own random seed, own control flow, 200 candidates x 8 chronological blocks) rather than touching Stage 6's own 50,000-draw loop at all -- it only reads the score/outcome arrays Stage 6 already collected. Computes the real combinatorial rank/logit statistic across all 70 balanced 4-vs-4 block splits. Net cost is actually LESS total compute than the search it's auditing. Diffed the whole thing before shipping: zero lines changed or removed anywhere outside the version banner, even though it lives in the same function as the fragile loop.

On "all stats as high as we can possibly get them" -- I want to flag one important distinction rather than just agree and move on: I'm reading this as "find real, generalizing edge the existing logic isn't using," not "loosen the PBO/CSCV/walk-forward guardrails to make backtests look better." Those guardrails exist specifically because this whole campaign started from an honest STAND DOWN caused by overfitting -- if "higher stats" and "actually generalizes" ever pull in different directions, generalizing wins. Saved this as a standing goal with that framing attached, so it doesn't get misread later as license to weaken anything.

I already have a concrete, legitimate candidate for this: the "P3B-bracket-recheck" anomaly from the 2026-07-17 log I found during the earlier log-mining pass -- a calibrated bracket scored 20% vs a simple fixed bracket's 38% on the SAME sample, and the code just regularizes instead of switching to the better one it already measured. That's real performance sitting unused. Haven't touched it yet this session (already very large) -- it's the natural next target whenever you want me to continue.

Attaching final SLS(84) mq4+ex4 and the updated forge_ledger.json (64 passes).
